He actively contributes to the cloud-native ecosystem by developing and sharing open-source AI and infrastructure projects, such as agentic frameworks and serverless CDK tools. Beyond his technical contributions, he serves as an AWS AI Hero and community mentor, frequently speaking at events to help organizations bridge the gap between cloud-native architecture and scalable AI systems.

person.name: Dave Zolotusky
person.bio:
Dave is an engineer in Spotify's Platform team, where his focus has been core infrastructure, the data platform, and Spotify's use of cloud services. A noted evangelist for cloud native and open source technologies, Dave spurred Spotify to join the CNCF and remain active in the community, launched the Stockholm CNCF meetup, and is both a CNCF Ambassador and a member of the KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Program Committee.

In his work at Spotify, Dave has been essential to the adoption of CNCF projects like Kubernetes, gRPC, Envoy, and OpenTelemetry, as well as experimentation with Service Mesh. Prior to his time with Spotify, Dave worked at AWS, at VMware on cloud management software, and at Microsoft on the Office team.

He has served as a mentor in the Google Summer of Code (GSoC) program, guiding contributors and fostering the growth of the open-source community. Shivanshu has presented his work at renowned global conferences, including AI_Dev, the GSoC Mentor Summit, IstioCon, and KubeCon, showcasing his expertise and commitment to advancing open-source innovation. He is available for connections on LinkedIn, Twitter, or through CNCF Slack.

person.name: Mike Bufano
person.bio: Mike is a Program Manager within Google’s Open Source Programs Office (OSPO), where he manages the strategic partnerships and foundation memberships that support Google’s open-source ecosystem. His work focuses on the intersection of ecosystem governance, corporate strategy, and community sustainability. Mike is also a dedicated proponent of driving diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives across the technology industry. Outside of work, he loves bringing people together over a home-cooked meal, hunting for the best karaoke spots, and exploring the unexpected corners of New York City.

person.name: Chase Christensen
person.bio: Chase Christensen is a Principal Customer Engineer at Wiz, where he helps organizations secure cloud-native and ML workloads. His journey into MLOps began through the Kubeflow community, where he went on to chair both the 2025 Kubeflow Summit in Atlanta (https://colocatedeventsna2025.sched.com/event/28FLh/kubeflow-summit-welcome-+-opening-remarks-kubeflow-summit-a-community-big-enough-for-everyone-chase-christensen-wiz)and the Kubeflow Virtual Symposium (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t0vfJY267f0&list=PLj6h78yzYM2MCEJPmVgUZsEx7lFjqh8BC&index=5).  Along the way, he authored the Linux Foundation’s Introduction to AI/ML Toolkits with Kubeflow course, helping practitioners bridge Kubernetes expertise with machine learning practice. Chase also founded the Applied Machine Learning Collective of the Rockies (https://amlcoftherockies.org/), a Denver-based community focused on advancing hands-on AI/ML collaboration. He shares his thoughts on MLOps and open-source adoption as a contributor to the Evil Tux blog (https://eviltux.com/2025/06/17/the-kubeflow-blog-series/).

person.name: Karapet Andreasyan

Joining the Kubestronaut program feels like a great opportunity to deepen my Kubernetes expertise and grow alongside others who share the same passion.

My work focuses on CNCF-aligned tools, GitOps, CI/CD automation, infrastructure as code, and building reliable, scalable, and secure production systems
